category_title: Students who exhibit frustration through crying and self-abusive behavior during long tasks
student_description: Shaun is an 11-year-old student who exhibits severe distress, including crying, garbled vocalizations, and self-abusive behavior, when overwhelmed by specific environmental factors. The evidence reveals he uniquely requires extreme task segmentation (one page at a time) and very close physical proximity (within 0.3 meters) from staff to maintain engagement and prevent behavioral escalation.

criteria.rationale: Shaun's specific triggers (distance, task volume) require continuous monitoring to ensure the environment remains adapted to his needs without resorting to punitive measures.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan includes tracking the relationship between environmental variables (like task presentation methods and physical proximity) and Shaun's behavioral responses to proactively adjust interventions.
